chunks.page_summary: This page details the process of integrating Rheonics sensors with an Industrial PC (IPC), a USR-W610 converter, and Ignition SCADA for remote data visualization via Wi-Fi and MQTT.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Rheonics Sensor Integration with IPC, USR-W610, and Ignition SCADA
Modified on: Wed, 22 Oct, 2025 at 6:57 PM

What is the purpose of this article?
This article explains the setup and configuration to send data of Rheonics sensors through Wi-Fi, and the Node-RED setup in the IPC for remote data visualization through Ignition SCADA.

What products are involved?
This article is based on using the Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME), an Industrial PC (IPC), and the USR-W610 to send sensor data through Wi-Fi to an MQTT Broker ar

1. Overview
This article outlines the steps to integrate Rheonics sensors with the USR-W610, which is an RS232/RS485 to Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter. The integration allows the sensors to connect to a network generated by an IPC. The IPC, which is connected to the internet via Ethernet, can then forward sensor data to an MQTT broker using Node-RED which allows for remote data visualization through Ignition SCADA.

Figure 1: Architecture of Rheonics SME, USR-W610, IPC, and Ignition SCADA integration

2. Prerequisites
* Rheonics SME with Modbus RTU (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005)
    * SME connects to the sensor probe and transmits the data using different industrial communication protocols.
    * (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) Modbus RTU (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) protocol is used in this article.
* Industrial PC (IPC)
    * Robust computer used in industrial environments.
* MQTT broker
    * A cloud-based MQTT message broker acts as an intermediary between clients that send (publish) and receive (subscribe) data. In this case, it transmits the sensor data received from Node-RED to Ignition for remote visualization.
* Ignition SCADA
    * An industrial platform that can connect to an MQTT broker, enabling real-time data visualization and interface creation for monitoring multiple devices.
* USR-W610
    * RS232/RS485 to Wi-Fi & Ethernet Converter that allows the Rheonics SME to connect to a Wi-Fi network and send data using
    * (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005) Modbus RTU (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217005)

3. USR-W610 Setup
The USR-W610 module is used to send serial data to a Wi-Fi network. In this case, the device is configured as STA, so it is connected to the Wi-Fi network generated by the IPC.
To configure the USR-W610 as STA and connect to a Wi-Fi network, refer to Connecting Rheonics Sensors to Wi-Fi Networks using USR-W610
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000419321)
(https://rheonics.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AwCTz)
1. Connect to the network generated by the IPC.
key_facts: The article describes the setup and configuration for sending Rheonics sensor data via Wi-Fi and Node-RED for remote visualization through Ignition SCADA.
The integration involves Rheonics Sensor Module Electronics (SME), an Industrial PC (IPC), and the USR-W610 converter.
The USR-W610 serves as an RS232/RS485 to Wi-Fi and Ethernet Converter, enabling Rheonics SME to connect to a Wi-Fi network.
The IPC, connected to the internet via Ethernet, forwards sensor data to an MQTT broker using Node-RED.
A cloud-based MQTT broker facilitates data transmission from Node-RED to Ignition for remote visualization.
Ignition SCADA is an industrial platform used for real-time data visualization and interface creation by connecting to the MQTT broker.
The Modbus RTU protocol is specifically used for data transmission between the Rheonics SME and other components.
The USR-W610 module is configured as an STA (station) to connect to the Wi-Fi network generated by the IPC.
Rheonics sensors can measure parameters such as density, viscosity, and temperature.
diagram_labels: Rheonics Sensors (e.g. SRV, SRD, DVP, others) for density, viscosity, temperature
Figure 1: Architecture of Rheonics SME, USR-W610, IPC, and Ignition SCADA integration

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for configuring the network settings of a USR-W610 converter, detailing its wiring with a Rheonics SME and sensors, and guiding the process of obtaining sensor data in Node-RED via Modbus TCP.

2. Once the USR-W610 is connected to the Wi-Fi network, do the wiring for serial communication as the following diagram.

Modbus RTU
Figure 3: Wiring diagram of USR-W610 to Rheonics SME

4. IPC Setup
The IPC used in this article counts with Windows 10 and makes it compatible with Node-RED, but you can refer to https://rheonics.atlassian.net/wiki/x/EYClyq Node-RED Setup and MQTT for Rheonics Sensors (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000418666) in case you are using another OS.

4.1. Node-RED installation
Node-RED is used for different applications and can be easily configured to implement data acquisition via Modbus TCP obtained from the USR-W610 and send it to an MQTT Broker.
To learn the basic steps for setting up Node-RED and using the modules mentioned in this article, visit the Node-RED Setup and MQTT for Rheonics Sensors (https://support.rheonics.com)

4.2. Obtaining Data in Node-RED
The goal is to obtain sensor data via Wi-Fi in Node-RED and send it to an MQTT broker.
1. The USR-W610 sends the data as Modbus TCP, so the node-red-contrib-modbus module in Node-RED is needed.
2. The configuration for the Modbus-client is as follows, using the IP address assigned to the USR-W610 during its configuration and the Modbus Address of the SME (default: 1)
key_facts: The USR-W610 is an RS232/485 to WiFi and Ethernet Converter.
Rheonics Sensors (e.g., SRV, SRD, DVP) are designed for measuring density, viscosity, and temperature.
The IPC mentioned in this article operates with Windows 10 and is compatible with Node-RED.
Node-RED can be easily configured for data acquisition via Modbus TCP from the USR-W610 and subsequently sending the data to an MQTT Broker.
The USR-W610 transmits data using Modbus TCP protocol.
The `node-red-contrib-modbus` module is required in Node-RED for Modbus TCP communication.
The Modbus-client configuration in Node-RED necessitates the IP address assigned to the USR-W610 and the Modbus Address of the SME (which defaults to 1).
diagram_labels: You could configure STA interface parameters and turn on/off AP+STA here.
RS232/485 To WiFi and Ethernet Converter
Rheonics Sensors (eg. SRV, SRD, DVP, others) for density, viscosity, temperature
Figure 3: Wiring diagram of USR-W610 to Rheonics SME

chunks.page_summary: This page details the process of integrating Rheonics sensor data, including Modbus client configuration, Node-RED flow setup for MQTT publishing, and data visualization using Ignition SCADA.
chunks.extracted_text:

Figure 4: modbus-client configuration

3. To send data from a Rheonics sensor to an MQTT broker, refer to Integrating Rheonics Sensors with Node-RED and MQTT for Remote Data Access (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000418653)

4. The node-RED flow to read viscosity, density, and temperature to send it to an MQTT broker would look as follows.

[Node-RED flow diagram elements]:
- viscosity -> Floatconverter -> debug 55
- viscosity -> Floatconverter -> debug 57
- viscosity -> Floatconverter -> srv/viscosity
- viscosity -> Floatconverter -> debug 56

- density -> Floatconverter -> debug 58
- density -> Floatconverter -> debug 62
- density -> Floatconverter -> srv/density
- density -> Floatconverter -> debug 59

- temperature -> Floatconverter -> debug 60
- temperature -> Floatconverter -> debug 63
- temperature -> Floatconverter -> srv/temperature
- temperature -> Floatconverter -> debug 61

Figure 5: Node-RED flow

5. Visualization through Ignition

Now that the Rheonics sensor data has been published into the MQTT broker, the parameters can be visualized remotely with Ignition through another device.

To learn how to connect Ignition SCADA to an MQTT broker to visualize Rheonics sensor data refer to Using Ignition SCADA to Visualize Rheonics Sensor Data via MQTT (https://s

key_facts: Rheonics sensor data can be sent to an MQTT broker for remote access.
Node-RED can be configured to read viscosity, density, and temperature data from Rheonics sensors and publish it to an MQTT broker.
Ignition SCADA is used to visualize Rheonics sensor data that has been published to an MQTT broker.
The document provides instructions for setting up a Modbus client configuration with specific network parameters.
Data visualization in Ignition shows real-time viscosity and temperature readings from a Rheonics Viscometer SRV.
